parsed_review.summary: CP00-642 (Risk A, 10 units, RP20.P08.M06.S11-S20, single "Synthetic Fixture Set" section) is a clean descriptor-only pack and is supported by its implementation, tests, and evidence. The contract descriptor (contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json:47218, generated by packages/data-room/src/service.js:5566) defines exactly 10 rows with deliverable types {implementation:8, hermes_evidence:1, test:1}, matching the baseline distribution and required_section_rows. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are exhaustively attested (synthetic_only, no_real_data, writes_product_state=false, evaluates_runtime_permission=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, executes_p08_hermes_evidence_synthetic_fixture_tail_runtime=false, full no_write_attestation all-false) and enforced by validators.js:4708 and validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs (deepEqual of contract projections vs registry). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-643; Claude packet is read-only (Read,Grep,Glob). CP178+ hardening blockers (auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) are present in the contract, requirements, and claude packet. Hermes patterns are used only for the descriptor-only evidence packet/receipt shape, not product runtime. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT yet asserted: manifest production_ready=false/status=in_progress, construction-inspection PENDING_REVIEW, adjudication pending, and claude-review-result pending_review with no prior valid/invalid attempts — promotion is properly gated on this review plus human final approval. Tests (model.test.js:1879-1916) and command-evidence (all commands exit 0, no_real_data=true, empty Hermes command_ids) corroborate. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic carryover noted. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver.

findings.title: Stale P05-named no-op flag carried into P08 closeout_handoff descriptor row
findings.details: The cp642 descriptor's closeout_handoff row carries executes_p05_fixture_closeout_runtime:false alongside handoff_descriptor_only:true. This field name originates from the P05 lineage and is inherited here because DATA_ROOM_VDR_CORE_CP642_ROW_EXTRAS spreads ...DATA_ROOM_VDR_CORE_CP641_ROW_EXTRAS (packages/data-room/src/service.js:3204-3227) without redefining closeout_handoff, and the row extra is applied at service.js:5548. For a P08 Hermes-evidence synthetic-fixture-tail pack the semantically scoped assertion would be P08-named. The value is false, so it only redundantly asserts that no runtime is opened — it does not weaken any gate, open runtime, use real data, write product state, or make a false production_ready/enterprise-trust claim. Because both the committed contract and the generated descriptor contain the identical field, the deepEqual validator (validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs:687) and test (model.test.js:1915) still pass.
findings.recommendation: Non-blocking. In a future maintenance pass, override the closeout_handoff entry in DATA_ROOM_VDR_CORE_CP642_ROW_EXTRAS (or normalize the shared row-extras chain) so the no-op flag reflects the P08 scope, or drop the stale P05-specific field. No change is required for CP00-642 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic note. Does not affect the production_ready promotion path. Pack remains eligible for closeout pending the required human final approval.
